trails.name: Alta Via 1 - Classic Dolomites Trek
trails.description: The most famous multi-day trek in the Dolomites, traversing stunning mountain landscapes from north to south through the heart of the Dolomites UNESCO World Heritage site.
highlights: Experience sunrise and sunset at high altitude
Immerse in alpine culture and cuisine

stages.name: Lago di Braies to Rifugio Sennes
stages.description: Begin your Dolomites adventure from the iconic Lago di Braies, climbing through alpine meadows to reach Rifugio Sennes with panoramic views.
highlights: Stunning views of Lago di Braies
First glimpse of the Sennes plateau

stops.name: Malga en route
stops.what: Cold drinks, local cheese, energy snacks

sleep.booking_note: Book 3–4 weeks ahead in high season. Phone preferred.

exit_routes.name: Descent to nearest valley
exit_routes.description: Retrace to the last waypoint and follow signs to the nearest valley settlement. Follow any red-white-red trail markers downhill. If lost, call mountain rescue: 118.
exit_routes.transport: SAD bus from nearest valley town — check sad.it for timetables.

rejoining_options.description: Return to yesterday's endpoint and continue
rejoining_options.how: Take local transport back to the rifugio you stayed at last night, rest, and continue the next stage fresh the following day.
Check with mountain rescue or local taxi whether a road approach to the next night's rifugio is possible from the valley.

stages.weather_risk: Check the morning forecast. Leave early — afternoon storms are common in the Dolomites from June to August. Turn back if you see anvil-shaped clouds building.

stages.name: Rifugio Sennes to Rifugio Fanes
stages.description: Traverse the scenic Sennes plateau before descending to the emerald Lago di Fanes and the cozy Rifugio Fanes.

stops.name: Malga en route
stops.what: Cold drinks, local cheese, energy snacks

stages.name: Rifugio Fanes to Rifugio Lavarella
stages.description: An easier day through the beautiful Fanes valley, following streams and alpine pastures to Rifugio Lavarella.

stages.name: Rifugio Lavarella to Rifugio Scotoni
stages.description: Climb to the dramatic Forcella Lavarella pass with breathtaking views, then descend to Rifugio Scotoni beneath the towering Tofane peaks.

stops.what: Cold drinks, local cheese, energy snacks

stages.name: Rifugio Scotoni to Cortina d'Ampezzo
stages.description: Final descent through forests and meadows, arriving in the famous resort town of Cortina d'Ampezzo - celebrate your Alta Via 1 completion!

booking_tips: Book all rifugios at least 2-3 weeks in advance, especially in July-August
Confirm bookings a few days before starting
Bring cash - not all rifugios accept cards
Half-board (dinner + breakfast) is recommended at rifugios
Check weather forecast before starting each stage

josephineNote.en: This is one of the great Dolomite classics. Do it in late September when the crowds are gone and the larch trees are golden — the light filtering through the larches near Rifugio Fanes is something you will carry with you for years.
josephineNote.it: Questa è una delle grandi classiche delle Dolomiti. Fatela a fine settembre, quando le folle sono sparite e i larici sono dorati — la luce che filtra tra i larici vicino al Rifugio Fanes è qualcosa che vi porterete dentro per anni.
josephineNote.de: Das ist einer der großen Dolomiten-Klassiker. Geht ihn Ende September, wenn die Menschenmassen weg sind und die Lärchen golden leuchten — das Licht, das bei der Rifugio Fanes durch die Lärchen fällt, ist etwas, das ihr jahrelang mit euch tragen werdet.

trails.booking_strategy: Book all rifugios 4–6 weeks ahead in July–August. Most accept phone or email; some have online booking. Cancel at least 48h ahead to free your spot.
trails.josephine_adventure_note: This is one of my favourite multi-day routes. Don't rush the first morning — the light on the Dolomites at 7am is something you'll remember for life.
